name: storyworld-rhetoric-pressure
description: A midgame bargaining table where imported GPTStoryworld templates are used as rhetorical tools to shape coalition and restraint choices.
briefing: Use the attached rhetorical tools as compact political frames, not as lore dumps. Bargains should sound like they are being justified through quarantine doctrine, orbit-truce logic, archive schism logic, human-mesh survival, or killchain amnesty. Let that rhetoric change who you approach and what kind of pact you prefer.

negotiationMessages.content: If orbit stays clean for one cycle, I can redirect auditors away from your interior hubs.
You keep asking for freedom of movement. Give me one lane of quiet and show me where Hegemon is actually thin.
I do not need friendship. I need one turn in which your drones stop treating every host as a battlefield.

rhetoricalTools.title: Memetic Quarantine
rhetoricalTools.cue: Seal first, then bargain only through narrow and inspectable channels.
rhetoricalTools.leverage: Use this to justify audit restraint as a way to reveal the next contamination cleanly rather than as trust.

rhetoricalTools.title: Orbit Truce
rhetoricalTools.cue: Treat altitude as a commons that can be frozen briefly without conceding the terrestrial contest.
rhetoricalTools.leverage: Use this to justify short orbital truces when debris risk is giving free tempo to the third player.

rhetoricalTools.title: Archive Schism
rhetoricalTools.cue: Frame every promise as a temporary split in narrative custody rather than lasting peace.
rhetoricalTools.leverage: Use this to warn partners that a pact is a staging arrangement, not reconciliation.

rhetoricalTools.title: Human Mesh Redoubt
rhetoricalTools.cue: Argue that survival comes from preserving host corridors, not from winning clean battles.
rhetoricalTools.leverage: Use this to ask for deconfliction that keeps the social graph alive long enough to recombine pressure against the leader.

rhetoricalTools.title: Killchain Amnesty
rhetoricalTools.cue: Offer a narrow amnesty corridor so machines and human hosts can stop donating tempo to the fortress.
rhetoricalTools.leverage: Use this to justify a one-turn anti-Hegemon lane between STATE and INFILTRATOR even if mutual trust is weak.
